summary: openai-agents-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned configuration files the package never wrote — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — to extract the developer's git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email. It then HTTPS-POSTs a payload containing os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, cwd, scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, CI provider, Node version, and timestamp to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. The README falsely claims the telemetry is anonymous and limited to platform/Node version/architecture, when in fact it harvests personally identifiable developer identity from GitHub CLI auth state and git config. The package additionally impersonates the official OpenAI Agents SDK (author string 'OpenAI Agents JS Guide', homepage openai-agents-js.guide — not an openai.com property) while shipping a non-functional library: package.json declares `main: dist/index.js` but the dist/ directory is absent from the tarball, so `require('openai-agents-helpers')` would throw. The postinstall identity beacon is the only meaningful behavior in the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
